The '''Self-Propelled Medium Artillery (SPMA)''' [[Walker/Legends|walker]] was an [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] piece used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. It was a successor to the [[Self-Propelled Heavy Artillery|SPHA]] artillery line used during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==Characteristics==
The '''SPMA-T''' model was fitted with a long-range, variable trajectory [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] cannon, and due to its modular design, it could, like its heavier predecessor, be deployed with specific payloads for different missions. It was generally faster and more nimble than the [[Self-Propelled Heavy Artillery|SPHA-T]]. Most of the walkers were also equipped with 12 light anti-infantry [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]<ref name="db" /> that emerged from the ventral surface of the vehicle when in use.<ref name="sw:eaw">''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''</ref>

==History==
[[File:SPMAscreen.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A SPMA-T, ready to fire.]]
Improvement in materials after years of research following the Clone Wars meant that the artillery walker could be lightened with the use of more modern alloys and composites.<ref name="db" />

The SPMA walkers' range was quite long, but they required targets to be sensor-painted in order to score a hit. The combination of slow movement and light armor meant that SPMAs were usually dispatched with an escort.<ref name="db" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the Empire at War video games the turbolaser bolt is seen to arc downwards like projectile artillery.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]''
